title: Executive Order 8890—Establishing the Office of Defense Health and Welfare Services in the Executive Office of the President and Defining its Functions and Duties

flags.justification: The Director's role is coordinative and advisory ('serve as the center for coordination... make available... study, plan, and encourage'), not substantive regulatory authority over third parties.
The Director serves under presidential direction and needs the President's approval to establish principal subdivisions (Sec. 4).
Multiple related service areas (health, welfare, nutrition, recreation) coherently serve one purpose: defense health and welfare coordination.
A purely coordinative/advisory body creates no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Authority is tied to 'the national emergency as declared by the President on May 27, 1941' with no reauthorization or review mechanism despite being emergency-contingent.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
No direct third-party-facing compliance regime is created by this coordinative body.
Routine administrative-coordination body, similar in character to other OEM sub-offices established the same period; no novel authority claim.

The order explicitly directs the Office to 'collaborate with and utilize... existing departments and agencies' and 'cooperate and work in conjunction with the Office of Civilian Defense' (Sec. 5) — coordination by design, not mandate redirection.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
